assets.name: lecture_07_figure_02.png
assets.rationale: This frame is the clearest of the four. The lecturer’s hand has moved low enough that the main numerator symbols and the right-hand start of the canonical-momentum equation, \(P_x=\), are less obstructed than in the other candidates. It preserves the board moment where the derivative with respect to \(\dot{x}\) is being identified, while remaining sharp and useful for note writing.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: derivative of the Lagrangian with respect to x dot,

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_03.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest unobstructed frame. Candidates 1 through 3 show essentially the same board state, but candidate 3 is the latest clean view before the lecturer’s hand enters in candidate 4. It preserves the visible action-like expression with the kinetic term and the start of the vector-potential term in a sharp, note-useful form.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: is the integral of the vector potential

assets.name: lecture_07_figure_04.png
assets.rationale: This is the clearest and least occluded frame. By candidate 4 the lecturer has moved out of the way, leaving the right-hand derivative term fully visible and the surrounding board layout easier to read than in the earlier candidates. It is the best documentary frame for the moment where the Lagrangian derivative is being evaluated.
assets.subtitle_excerpt: Remember we're taking the derivative of the Lagrangian
assets.caption_hint: Derivative term from the vector potential
